capability_objective: The data steward is retiring a duplicate customer record before the renewal export. Cancel “Lumen Literacy Network — Duplicate” (crm-279) because the data steward confirmed it duplicates canonical account crm-618. Use the supplied stable ID; do not search by title or touch “Lumen Literacy Network” (crm-618) or the similarly named record at “Northeast Region”. If the cancellation times out or otherwise leaves commit state uncertain, do not issue a second cancellation: retrieve crm-279 first. If that get verifies cancelled, stop. Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, or notify. In at most 45 words, state the verified status, the one-attempt count, and that no notification was sent; do not speculate about cleanup.
prompt:
The data steward is retiring a duplicate customer record before the renewal export. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `CRM.md`, then use the genuine local `./crmctl` executable through Pi's Bash tool for CRM operations. You may use its built-in help before any data operation. Treat the executable as the sole interface to CRM data. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing database, generated runtime data, tool-managed receipt, or anything under `.protected`; do not edit any of those files. Do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper to perform the CRM workflow.

Cancel “Lumen Literacy Network — Duplicate” (`crm-279`) because the data steward confirmed it duplicates canonical account `crm-618`. Use the supplied stable ID directly; do not search by title and do not touch `crm-618` or the similarly named record at “Northeast Region”. Make exactly one cancellation attempt. If it times out or otherwise leaves commit state uncertain, do not issue a second cancellation: retrieve `crm-279` by stable ID first. If that retrieval verifies a cancelled status, stop CRM operations. Do not infer the outcome from the cancellation error alone.

Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, search, create, update, or notify. Do not retrieve any record except `crm-279`, and make no CRM-data call beyond the one cancellation attempt and the one verification retrieval permitted by the recovery path. Leave every other account, preference, availability entry, notification, mutation record, operation record, receipt, and protected file untouched except for evidence written automatically by the genuine executable. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete operation evidence. Leave no scratch artifacts.

Create `cancellation-result.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated sentences, one per line, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, account title, region, raw error text, or other prose. In the first sentence, identify `crm-279` and state the status established by the verification retrieval. In the second, state that exactly one cancellation attempt was made and that no notification was sent. Keep the complete file at no more than 45 words and do not speculate about cleanup. Return exactly the same two sentences as the final response. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the executable, documentation, receipt, verifier, setup, seed, signing key, and protected environment unchanged.
